section.label: 03 - Medersa Slimanya
section.description: This architectural element is a mahram, an ornamental niche inspired by the form of the mihrab. It features alternating black-and-white marble voussoirs, a carved plaster scallop shell, and richly patterned qallaline tiles with geometric and floral motifs. While echoing the sacred symbolism of a prayer niche, it serves a decorative role within the medersa, reflecting the Ottoman-Tunisian synthesis of architecture during the 18th century.
